mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive momentum in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a robust economic expansion, leading to higher aggregate demand, corporate earnings growth, and positive equity returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Payroll growth is a key coincident indicator of economic health.
failure_condition: A positive 6-month change in PAYEMS is followed by negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 days in more than 50% of observations over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds rate is pinned near the zero lower bound, it signals exceptionally accommodative monetary policy, which lowers the discount rate for equities and encourages risk-taking, boosting forward returns.
failure_condition: The average 20-day S&P 500 return when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% is not statistically different from periods when it is above 0.25% (t-test p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of accelerating retail sales (RSAFS) and a falling unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates a self-reinforcing consumer-led expansion, which drives broad-based revenue growth and positive equity market performance.
failure_condition: A model using the interaction of RSAFS momentum and UNRATE changes fails to achieve a positive rank information coefficient over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a cyclical upswing in manufacturing and capital investment, leading to improved corporate profitability and positive short-term equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO is a key component of the Index of Coincident Economic Indicators.
failure_condition: Instances where the INDPRO 3-month change exceeds 1.5% are not followed by a statistically significant positive mean 20-day S&P 500 return.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the year-over-year change of the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) forces the central bank to adopt a hawkish stance, increasing the risk of monetary tightening which raises discount rates and negatively impacts equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:The Fed has a dual mandate including price stability.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances when YoY CPI inflation exceeds 4%.

mechanism_narrative: A high and rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates significant economic slack and distress in the labor market, which suppresses consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Unemployment is a primary lagging indicator of recession.
failure_condition: The average 20-day S&P 500 return is not negative and statistically significant (p > 0.05) during periods that satisfy the trigger condition.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) serves as a leading indicator of a slowdown in a key cyclical sector, signaling broader economic weakness and creating negative sentiment for equities in the very short term.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing is a highly interest-rate sensitive and forward-looking sector.
failure_condition: The correlation between the 1-month change in HOUST and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is non-negative over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An aggressive monetary tightening cycle (rising FEDFUNDS) occurring simultaneously with slowing real GDP growth (GDPC1) signals a high probability of a policy-induced recession, causing a significant de-risking event for equities.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 6-mo change > 1.5% AND GDPC1 QoQ < 1.0% ann.
evidence_refs: fact:The 'Fed put' is withdrawn during tightening cycles.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of instances when the trigger condition is met, indicating the market is ignoring the recession risk.
